factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with net revenues increasing 13% year-over-year, driven by acquisitions and foreign exchange benefits, but organic demand declined 1%.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2021 increased by 13% as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2020. The increase was the result of net acquisition/divestiture contribution of 12% and a foreign exchange benefit of 1%, partially offset by an organic decline of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Net revenues increased, but organic demand declined, indicating moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with a 25% increase to $2,578.7 at March 31, 2021, driven by organic growth and acquisitions.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 25% to $2,578.7 at March 31, 2021 as compared to $2,070.3 at March 31, 2020, organic growth was 3% and acquisitions contributed 22%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant backlog growth driven by organic and acquisition contributions.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Application Software segment, which saw a gross margin increase to 69.1%.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased to 69.1% in the first quarter of 2021 as compared to 66.7% in the first quarter of 2020 due primarily to the acquisition of Vertafore and favorable revenue mix across the segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments improving gross margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with a 1% decline in organic revenues year-over-year.

evidence.text: The increase was the result of net acquisition/divestiture contribution of 12% and a foreign exchange benefit of 1%, partially offset by an organic decline of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue decline indicates a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are declining in certain segments, particularly in the Process Technologies segment, which saw an 8% revenue decrease.

evidence.text: Our Process Technologies segment revenues decreased by 8% to $130.8 in the first quarter of 2021 as compared to $142.2 in the first quarter of 2020. Organic revenues decreased 10%, partially offset by a foreign exchange contribution of 2%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes in certain segments.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies, with growth in healthcare and government contracting markets offset by declines in education and upstream oil and gas markets.

evidence.text: The increase in organic revenues was due primarily to our businesses serving the healthcare and government contracting markets, partially offset by declines in our businesses serving the education markets. Organic revenues decreased due to broad-based revenue declines across the segment led by lower demand at our businesses serving upstream oil and gas end markets.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across different end markets.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, particularly in the Application Software segment, which saw a 26.7% operating margin.

evidence.text: The resulting operating margin was 26.7% in the first quarter of 2021 as compared to 24.1% in the first quarter of 2020. Gross margin increased to 69.1% in the first quarter of 2021 as compared to 66.7% in the first quarter of 2020 due primarily to the acquisition of Vertafore and favorable revenue mix across the segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins, particularly in Application Software.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is cautious, with ongoing disruptions from the COVID-19 pandemic affecting certain operations.

evidence.text: The spread of COVID-19 has caused us to modify our business practices, and we may take further actions as required by governmental and other regulatory authorities or as we determine to protect the safety or best interests of our employees, customers, suppliers and business partners.
evidence.subsection: Recently Issued Accounting Standards / Impact of COVID-19 on our Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing disruptions from COVID-19 pose a moderate risk.

factors.summary: Management is cautious regarding future prospects due to geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties could adversely affect our business prospects. The COVID-19 pandemic has had, and may continue to have, an adverse impact on our business.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution due to geopolitical and economic uncertainties, including the COVID-19 pandemic, indicating moderate concerns.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, geopolitical tensions, and economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties could adversely affect our business prospects. The COVID-19 pandemic has had, and may continue to have, an adverse impact on our business. A significant terrorist attack, other global conflict, or public health crisis could cause changes in world economies that would adversely affect us.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, geopolitical tensions, and economic uncertainties, posing moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on reducing debt and pursuing acquisitions to drive growth.

evidence.text: Although we maintain an active acquisition program we are currently focused on reducing debt. Future acquisitions will be dependent on numerous factors and it is not feasible to reasonably estimate if or when any such acquisitions will occur and what the impact will be on our business, financial condition and results of operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategy focusing on reducing debt and pursuing acquisitions, which indicates a positive outlook for growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, economic conditions, and foreign exchange rates.

evidence.text: Such risks and uncertainties include the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic on our business, operations, financial results and liquidity, including the duration and magnitude of such effects, which will depend on numerous evolving factors that we cannot accurately predict or assess, including: the duration and scope of the pandemic generally and in the geographical markets that we serve or in which we operate; the negative impact on global and regional markets, economies and economic activity; actions governments, businesses and individuals take in response to the pandemic; the effects of the pandemic, including all of the foregoing, on our employees, customers, suppliers, and business partners, and how quickly economies and demand for our products and services recover following the pandemic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include the significant impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, economic conditions, and foreign exchange rates, indicating substantial risks.

factors.summary: Capital expenditure outlook is stable, with expectations for similar levels as prior years.

evidence.text: We expect the aggregate of capital expenditures and capitalized software expenditures for the balance of the year to be comparable to prior years as a percentage of revenues.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The capital expenditure outlook is stable, with expectations for similar levels as prior years, showing no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving, with net debt decreasing and total net capital remaining stable.

evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is improving, with net debt decreasing and total net capital remaining stable, indicating positive developments.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payments and repatriation of earnings.

evidence.text: We intend to repatriate substantially all historical and future earnings. We expect existing cash balances, together with cash generated by our operations and amounts available under our credit facility, will be sufficient to fund our operating requirements for the foreseeable future.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payments and repatriation of earnings, signaling positive sentiment towards shareholders.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with a 54% increase in net cash provided by operating activities.

evidence.text: Operating activities - Net cash provided by operating activities increased by 54% to $559.6 in the three months ended March 31, 2021 as compared to $363.9 in the three months ended March 31, 2020, due primarily to higher net income net of non-cash expenses and growth in our software businesses, which generated cash from working capital in the quarter.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow generation has significantly improved, with a 54% increase in net cash provided by operating activities, indicating very positive developments.

factors.summary: M&A activity is focused on reducing debt and pursuing accretive acquisitions.

evidence.text: Although we maintain an active acquisition program we are currently focused on reducing debt. Future acquisitions will be dependent on numerous factors and it is not feasible to reasonably estimate if or when any such acquisitions will occur and what the impact will be on our business, financial condition and results of operations. Such acquisitions may be financed by the use of existing credit lines, future cash flows from operations, future divestitures, the proceeds from the issuance of new debt or equity securities or any combination of these methods, the terms and availability of which will be subject to market and economic conditions generally.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The focus on reducing debt and uncertainty around future acquisitions indicate a cautious approach, suggesting moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange exposure is favorable, contributing to revenue growth in certain segments.

evidence.text: The increase was the result of net acquisition/divestiture contribution of 12% and a foreign exchange benefit of 1%, partially offset by an organic decline of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to three months ended March 31, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange benefits contribute to revenue growth, indicating a favorable tailwind.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, including conflicts and trade tensions, pose potential disruptions and uncertainties.

evidence.text: Current geopolitical and economic uncertainties could adversely affect our business prospects. A significant terrorist attack, other global conflict, or public health crisis could cause changes in world economies that would adversely affect us.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential disruptions from geopolitical conflicts and trade tensions pose significant risks to business prospects.
